titleStmt.title: SII 16.28: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 28. (A.R. No. 496 of 1906.) MŌPŪRU, PULIVENDLA TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab lying behind the western gōpura of the Bhairavēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka 1347, Viśvāvasu, Āśvija śu. 3, Sunday, corresponding to 1425 A.D., September 15, °14.

It records that Tirumalarāya, son of Gaṇḍaragūḷi Boppadēvani Virūpaṇṇa, described as the nijabhṛitya of the king, had the gōpura to the west of the temple of Bhairavadēva of Mrōṁpūru constructed by his servant Vēmula Tippi-nāyaka, for the merit of his mothers Dēmāyamma and Nāgāyamma and his sister Tirumaladēvamma.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śā
livāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1347 Agunēṁṭi vi
śvāvasu saṁvatsara Āśvīja śu 3 Ā . svasti śrīma
drājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara Arirāya
vibhāḷa bhāsagetappuvarāyaragaṁḍa hiṁ
dūrāyasurattāṇu pūrvvapaścimadakṣiṇō
ttaracatussamudrādhīśvaruṁḍaina śrī vīravijaya
bukkarāya mahārāyala garbharatnākara
dhaṁrmmacāritra satkaḷābhōja vivēkinārā
yaṇa satyahariścaṁdra Udayabālabhāska
ruṁḍaina śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara śrī vīrapra
tāpa kumāra dēvarāyamahārāyalu vija
yānagara paṭṭaṇamaṁdu sukhasaṁgatā
vinōdaṁbuna rājyamu cēyucuṁḍagā
nu Ā dēvarāyamahārāyala nijabhṛtyu
ṁḍaina svastiśrīmadgaṁḍaragūḷi gajasihya
mēḷavikāmaṁḍalīkaragaṁḍa birudāṁkkulaina
boppadēvani virupaṁṇaṁgāri kumāruṁḍu
tirumalarāyaniṁgāru tama tallulu dēmā
ya(ṁ)mmaṁgārikinni nāgāya(ṁ)mmaṁgārikinni
ma Akka tirumaldēva(ṁ)mmaṁgārikinni puṇyamugā
nu mrōṁpū ri bhairavadēvarasannidhini paścimabhāgamaṁdu vū is written above the line.
tama baṁṭu vēmula tippināyanicētanu Ācaṁ
drārkkasthāyigānu kaṭṭiṁccina gōpuramu .
śākē śailajalatricaṁdragaṇitē viśvāvasōrvvatpa
rē . māsē cāśvayujē ca bhairavapuraḥ śubhrētarē The stone was too heavy to be turned over for the continuation of the
Digital edition of SII 16.28 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
